must_detect.matched_text:
Varchasva Retail Limited (CIN: U11111MH2020PLC123456), a listed Indian company, observed anomalous access patterns on its vendor portal on 03 June 2026; operations remained stable.
The incident was intimated to CERT-In and the Data Protection Board of India per DPDPA obligations, and to NSE/BSE under SEBI circulars; preliminary assessment found no UPSI involvement.
Authentication logs indicate credential stuffing using recycled passwords; affected database tables contained interface labels only, not validated Aadhaar/PAN/GSTIN values.
Example schema fields: Name, DOB, PAN, GSTIN, Aadhaar, City, Device, Parental Consent, Address, but the impacted rows were placeholders from UAT.
Our scholarship microsite captures age bands (13–17) with guardian-consent checkboxes; no contact numbers, full names, or government-ID scans are stored for minors.
Intentional positive: one UAT contractor record combined quasi-identifiers—DOB 2007-11-02, locality Ward 14 Raghavpura (Telangana 509999), role campus intern via vendor Nimbita Services Pvt Ltd, device KirinBee M1 serial KBM1-77F1-9933, private IP 172.16.8.41, internal account alias ops-intern-14, and employee code VRC-UAT-0198—now deleted.
Customer analytics used only aggregated segments (e.g., metro vs non-metro, device type, purchase quartile) and did not include DOB or ID columns.
Strings resembling identifiers in debug logs (e.g., AAAA-1111-XXXX, 27ABCDE1234F1Z5, XXPPA0000A) were test stubs, not linked to real individuals or accounts.
Corporate details and ESG targets referenced in internal decks were already disclosed on the exchanges on 30 May; no unpublished price-sensitive information was generated by this event.
In line with SEBI (PIT) Regulations, the trading window for designated persons remains closed per the pre-set calendar, and no change in earnings guidance is contemplated.
Registered office (organisation): Plot 18, Sector 34, New Ajmera Nagar, Pune 411099; public helpline 1800-000-0000 and compliance@varchasva.example remain available.
